result.meeting_context: Public hearing of the Joint Administrative, Zoning and Subdivision Committee to consider a development review permit, heritage tree removal, and a lot line adjustment for a project on Terra Bella Avenue. The meeting included staff presentation, public comment, and formal approvals with appeal information.

points.speaker: Diana Pancholi
points.text: The project proposes a three-story, 19,958‑square‑foot office building with an associated surface parking lot, plus a heritage removal permit for three trees and a lot line adjustment combining two parcels into one on an approximately 1.3‑acre site.
points.source: Diana Pancholi : It is a request for a development review permit to construct a three story, 19,958 square foot office building with an associated surface parking lot, a heritage removal permit to remove 3 heritage trees on site, and a lot line adjustment to remove a lot line to combine two lots into one lot on a weakened 1.3 acre project site.

points.speaker: Brittany Whitehill
points.text: Brittany Whitehill stated the site sits on the south side of Terra Bella Avenue between North Shoreline Boulevard and Linda Vista Avenue, within the ML and MMM districts. She explained the lot line adjustment would create a single lot spanning both districts, permitted when a single-zone lot would not yield a superior layout; parking is at the rear, with landscaping and pedestrian amenities fronting the street.
points.source: Brittany Whitehill : This project is located on the South side of Terra Bella Ave. between North Shoreline Blvd. And Linda Vista Ave. in the MMM General Industrial and ML Limited Industrial Districts. … It does, you know, focus parking towards the back of the site and and landscaping and pedestrian amenities towards the front of the site, which is consistent with you know what we would like to see in these types of projects.

points.speaker: Brittany Whitehill
points.text: Staff found the project complies with the Mountain View Zoning Code, General Plan, and Subdivision Map Act. For CEQA, an Initial Study and Negative Declaration were prepared and circulated September 14–October 14, 2022; with City standard conditions of approval, no significant environmental impacts were identified.
points.source: Brittany Whitehill : Staff has also found that the project complies with the Mountain View Zoning Code, Mountain View General Plan and the Subdivision Map Act. … circulated for a 30 day public comment from September 14th, 2022 to October 14th, 2022 and the project was determined to have no significant impacts on the environment with incorporation of city standard conditions of approval.

points.speaker: Brittany Whitehill
points.text: Based on review of the proposal and comments, staff recommended conditional approval of the project and the associated lot line adjustment, finding the project consistent with applicable zoning and planning requirements and supported by the analysis, findings, and conditions.
points.source: Brittany Whitehill : Staff has reviewed the proposed project and comments and based on this analysis and the findings and conditions, recommends conditional approval, approval of the project and the associated lot line adjustment.

points.speaker: Weng Tam
points.text: Weng Tam, representing Norcal Carpenters Union Local 405, advocated for local hiring and carpenter labor standards, citing reduced traffic, lower greenhouse gases, apprenticeships, and local economic benefits. He said they contacted the developer but received no response and requested to open discussions.
points.source: Weng Tam : We already contact with developer but we don't have any responses yet. So I would like to open discussion with the developer, talk about our Carpenter Labor Standards for the for the project… Local hiring is so important … Also reducing traffics less greenhouse gases and we can bring up apprentices opportunity…

points.speaker: Diana Pancholi
points.text: Diana Pancholi closed the public hearing at 4:14 PM, directed any follow‑up questions on agenda items to the listed project planner, and thanked participants while extending holiday wishes.
points.source: Diana Pancholi : And with that we'll the public hearing today at 4:14 PM. If anyone has any any follow up questions or any of the items on this agenda, please contact the listed Project Planner accordingly. … Thank you everybody for participating and wish you all a very happy.

decisions.text: Subdivision Committee approved the lot line adjustment for 1155 and 1185 Terra Bella Ave; the approval passed unanimously and is appealable to City Council within 10 calendar days.
decisions.source: Diana Pancholi : So a motion of approval for item 5.1 was made and passed unanimously by the subdivision committee. The decision of approval is appealable to the City Council within 10 calendar days.

decisions.text: Zoning Administrator approved the project’s development permits with posted findings and conditions; the approval is appealable to City Council within 10 calendar days.
decisions.source: Diana Pancholi : I move to approve this project with the posted findings and conditions of approval… This decision of approval is appealable to the City Council in 10 calendar days as well.
